lessons.text: Stand on the wisdom of those before you.
Step up to set things right when they slip.
See clearly, and you stop repeating the same mistakes.
Let understanding dissolve your fear and anger.
People meet you the way you approach them.
If you want results, act—don't just wish.
Do the work without letting it define you.
Know when to act and when to hold back.
Even doing nothing is a choice you're making.
Act from clarity, not from craving.
Act without clinging to how it turns out.
Be content with what comes; chase nothing.
Selfless work leaves no weight behind.
Bring your full presence to whatever you do.
Master your impulses instead of obeying them.
Give something of yourself, or gain nothing.
Nothing worthwhile comes without effort.
Understanding matters more than going through the motions.
Ask, listen, and stay humble enough to learn.
See yourself in others, and the walls come down.
Understanding can carry you past any mistake.
Insight loosens the grip of the past.
Nothing clears you like real understanding.
Commit fully, stay disciplined, and peace follows.
Endless doubt drains every joy.
Cut through your doubts and get moving.
Better to engage than to withdraw.
Free yourself from both craving and resentment.
Thinking and doing are one path, not two.
Start by doing; wisdom grows from practice.
Master yourself first, then act freely.
Notice your reactions instead of becoming them.
Do your part, then let results roll off you.
Work to grow, not just to gain.
Let go of the payoff and find peace.
Stay inwardly still even while you act.
Don't take all the credit or all the blame.
Understanding clears confusion like sunrise.
Give yourself fully to what matters most.
Treat everyone as equally worthy.

lessons.note: Only content is resting the mind in Brahman to transcend rebirth—pure metaphysics, and overlaps modernizable mind-rest lessons (132, 134).

lessons.text: Don't swing with every high and low.
Find joy within, not in things outside you.
Chasing quick pleasures ends in pain.
Ride out desire and anger before they rule you.
Work for the good of others.
A quiet mind sets you free.
Steady your breath to steady your focus.
Govern your mind, and fear loses its grip.
See the world as friendly, and rest easier.
Do what's yours to do, results aside.
Drop the selfish motive, not the work.
Lift yourself up; don't let yourself sink.
Be your own ally, not your own worst critic.
Stay level through praise and blame alike.
Gold or dirt—don't let either move you.
Meet friend and rival with the same calm.
Spend quiet time alone, free of wanting.
Set up a simple, steady space to focus.
Focus on one thing to clear your mind.
Sit tall and still to settle the mind.
Sit calm and fearless, focused on your aim.
A steady mind brings lasting calm.
Eat, sleep, and work in moderation.
Let the mind rest, free of wanting.
Keep your focus steady as a sheltered flame.
Contentment comes from knowing yourself.
Find a joy the senses can't give or take.
Find inner steadiness, and grief can't topple you.
Practice with resolve and without despair.
Drop the wants your imagination invents.
Quiet the mind gradually, one step at a time.
When your mind wanders, gently bring it back.
A calm mind opens the way to deep joy.
See yourself in everyone, and never feel alone.

lessons.note: Reduces to worshipping the one presence in all beings—religious framing that adds nothing beyond kept connection lessons (87, 107, 142, 144).

lessons.text: Feel others' joy and pain as your own.
Admit it: the mind is restless and hard to hold.
Tame a restless mind with practice and letting go.
Self-control makes the impossible reachable.
No honest effort is ever wasted.
What you build now carries into what's next.
Keep going; progress compounds over time.
Steady practice beats just knowing about it.
